dsl_rule.translation_notes: Flip-flop logic implemented using Bollinger Bands (50-period SMA ± 2.0 std dev) as proxy for upper/lower thresholds. Entry occurs on threshold crossing in respective direction; exit occurs on opposite threshold crossing (hard reversal). Mutually exclusive states maintained through OR logic between long and short conditions. High hysteresis achieved through 4 std dev band separation inherent in Bollinger Band construction.

backtest_rule.reason: Flip-flop logic implemented using Bollinger Bands (50-period SMA ± 2.0 std dev) as proxy for upper/lower thresholds. Entry occurs on threshold crossing in respective direction; exit occurs on opposite threshold crossing (hard reversal). Mutually exclusive states maintained through OR logic between long and short conditions. High hysteresis achieved through 4 std dev band separation inherent in Bollinger Band construction.

source_rule_text: Use flip-flop logic with mutually exclusive threshold crossings to generate binary state transitions: maintain a directional signal until the opposite threshold is crossed in the opposite direction.
